task: Network topology analysis of cascading failure vulnerability in sports systems
key_results: Scale-free networks 57% more vulnerable than regular networks (Network Vulnerability Index 1.24 vs 0.79); immediate recovery prevents cascades, delayed recovery (≥2 steps) triggers exponential propagation; 43% of phase space failure-dominant for scale-free
comments: Quantifies topology-dependent failure thresholds via network-agent model; empirical validation against professional injury/shutdown data demonstrates predictive utility for sports management.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Scale-free network topology increases cascading failure vulnerability compared to regular structures
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; 57% higher vulnerability, 1.24 vs 0.79 index; immediate intervention (rt=1) prevents cascades

hypotheses.hypothesis: Recovery delay thresholds determine cascade onset and propagation rate
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; delayed recovery (rt≥2) triggers exponential propagation; empirically validated

title: Design optimization and stiffness-equivalent method for an integrated starter generator in aerospace applications
task: Lightweight design optimization of integrated starter generator via stiffness-equivalent method and PyAnsys
key_results: Computational framework validates stiffness-equivalent method against fine 3D FEA; lightweight designs achieved while meeting operational constraints; applicable to aerospace mechanical properties database
comments: Novel application of stiffness-equivalent compensation for dummy structures; relative simplicity and computational efficiency demonstrated, but quantitative weight reduction not specified.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Stiffness-equivalent method accurately compensates for dummy structures in lightweight optimization
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; validation via 3D FEA; method demonstrated appropriate for aerospace applications

title: A unified deep learning framework integrating OpenStreetMap for multi-domain urban planning tasks
task: Multi-domain urban planning via unified deep learning with OpenStreetMap integration
key_results: Land-use classification 91.6% accuracy, building footprint 94.0%, traffic RMSE 3.6 vehicles/hour, air-quality RMSE 2.3 µg/m³
comments: Scalable integration of OSM, satellite, and demographic-environmental data across multiple tasks, though real-world scalability beyond Krasnodar is not demonstrated.

title: PCB-YOLOV8X: a network for detecting micro-sized defects on PCB surfaces based on enhanced feature information
task: PCB surface micro-defect detection via YOLOv8 with adaptive feature extraction and enhanced loss function
key_results: mAP@0.5 98.44% (+2.76% vs YOLOv8), Precision 97.67% (+2.33%), Recall 96.34% (+2.59%); C2f-DCNV2 adaptive features + SPPF-LSKA enhancement + IWD-CIoU loss
comments: Specialized architecture for micro-defects via heterogeneous convolution kernels and partial convolution filtering; substantial improvements over baseline, though evaluation limited to PCB domain.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Adaptive feature extraction + enhanced loss function improves micro-defect detection over standard YOLOv8
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; 2.76% mAP improvement, reduced missed detections and false alarms

title: Advanced channel estimation in OTFS and NOMA using deep bayesian gaussian processes and compressive sensing
task: Channel estimation in OTFS-NOMA systems via Deep Bayesian Gaussian Processes and compressive sensing
key_results: NMSE 0.01447 at 12 dB (90% lower than MMSE-CE); BER 0.021159 at 12 dB; 50% reduction in pilot overhead; tested on 100 users at 120 km/h (1,112 Hz Doppler)
comments: Integration of DNNs, Gaussian Processes, and compressive sensing effectively handles high-mobility Doppler shifts and multipath; demonstrates scalability across mobility and channel complexity.

model_io.model: DBGP-CS (Deep Bayesian GP with Compressive Sensing)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Deep Bayesian GP with compressive sensing reduces pilot overhead and improves channel estimation under high mobility
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; 90% NMSE improvement over MMSE, 50% pilot reduction achieved on ETU channel at 120 km/h

title: Review of large YOLOv8 and RT-DETR energy efficiency on edge devices for real-time detection
task: Energy-efficient edge deployment of large YOLOv8 and RT-DETR object detectors
key_results: Neither large-model variant achieves strict 25 FPS real-time on tested edge platforms; deployment efficiency determined jointly by nominal compute, memory behavior, runtime overhead, and quantization sensitivity
comments: Comprehensive benchmarking reveals that FLOPs alone do not predict edge efficiency; further optimization or smaller models required for true real-time performance.

model_io.model: YOLOv8l and RT-DETR-l with various runtimes

hypotheses.hypothesis: Model-specific runtime optimization (TensorRT) provides better deployment efficiency than nominal FLOPs prediction
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; TensorRT strongest for Jetson Orin NX; relative ranking depends on runtime realization

title: Dismantling complex networks based on higher-order graph neural network
task: Network dismantling via higher-order graph neural network representation learning
key_results: HoGNN framework with SPR (Structural and Processual Role-aware Network Dismantling) model achieves superior dismantling efficiency; minimal node removals required to collapse networks with multi-dimensional higher-order structures
comments: Framework flexibly integrates diverse higher-order relationships from macro/micro perspectives; empirical validation on real and synthetic networks, though scalability to very large networks not addressed.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Multi-dimensional higher-order representations identify critical nodes missed by lower-order analysis
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; SPR exhibits superior dismantling efficiency on both synthetic and real networks

title: Room temperature collective blinking and photon bunching from CsPbBr3 quantum dot superlattice
task: Demonstrating room-temperature collective blinking and photon bunching in perovskite QD superlattices
key_results: CsPbBr3 superlattices exhibit two-level blinking and photon bunching (degree up to 3.9) at room temperature; long-range exciton migration to localized trap; biexciton-exciton cascade origin
comments: First observation of photon bunching in perovskite QDs at room temperature is significant; scalability to larger superlattices and deterministic trap placement warrant investigation.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Perovskite QD superlattices support room-temperature collective states with photon bunching
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; photon bunching degree up to 3.9 observed

title: Role of fragility of the glass formers in the yielding transition under oscillatory shear
task: Understanding yielding transition dependence on glass fragility and annealing
key_results: Fragile glasses show yield-point increases with annealing below critical degree, while strong glasses remain constant; universal behavior across metallic, molecular, granular, and network-forming glasses
comments: The modified mean-field elastoplastic model elegantly connects energy barriers to yielding, though experimental validation of the fragility-dependent mechanism would strengthen the claims.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Yielding behavior in aged glasses depends on material fragility
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: fragile and strong glasses exhibit distinct age-dependence patterns

title: A general purposed machine learning interatomic potential for Mg-Al-Si-O system suitable for Earth materials at high pressure and temperature conditions
task: Developing machine learning interatomic potential for Mg-Al-Si-O system at high P-T conditions
key_results: r2SCAN functional with pairwise Gaussian correction reduces energy error from 5.2 to 1.2 kJ/mol; predicted isochemical phase diagrams agree with experiments; solid-melt interfacial anisotropy: 6% for periclase, 12% for forsterite
comments: The ML potential enables efficient thermodynamic calculations; generalization to broader compositional space and validation against additional experimental data would strengthen applicability.

model_io.model: Machine learning interatomic potential (r2SCAN + Gaussian correction)

hypotheses.hypothesis: ML potential with r2SCAN training data accurately reproduces Earth material phase relations
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; good agreement between predicted and experimental phase diagrams

title: Improved strategies for fermionic quantum simulation with global interactions
task: Optimizing fermionic excitation circuits for ion trap quantum computers with Mølmer-Sørensen gates
key_results: 2× and 4× reductions in MS gates for single and double excitations respectively compared to previous schemes; improved parallelism and reduced circuit depth
comments: The approach elegantly exploits MS gate structure for fermionic operations; practical error reduction gains require characterization on real hardware.

model_io.model: Ion trap quantum computer with MS interactions

hypotheses.hypothesis: MS gates naturally suit non-local fermionic excitation operators
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; 2-4× gate reductions achieved

title: Symmetry driven altermagnetic spin splitting in hexagonal CrTe from first principles
task: Identifying altermagnetic spin splitting in hexagonal CrTe from first-principles calculations
key_results: CrTe exhibits altermagnet characteristics with collinear magnetic order and substantial spin splitting while maintaining zero net magnetization without spin-orbit coupling
comments: Reinterpretation of CrTe from conventional AFM to altermagnet is significant for spintronic applications; experimental verification through spin transport measurements would be valuable.

model_io.model: DFT density functional theory with spin polarization

hypotheses.hypothesis: CrTe exhibits altermagnet behavior with symmetry-protected spin polarization despite zero magnetization
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; significant spin splitting observed in collinear AFM configuration

title: Copper oxide tailors multifunctional properties of fluorobarioborate glasses for optical dielectric and shielding applications
task: Investigating CuO doping effects on optical, dielectric, and radiation-shielding properties of fluorobarioborate glasses
key_results: Enhanced UV absorption with increased CuO; optical band gap 3.48–3.60 eV (direct ≈ indirect); improved γ-ray attenuation with higher CuO; dielectric behavior shows hopping mechanisms
comments: Comprehensive multifunctional material characterization; the simultaneous optical clarity and shielding properties are promising for applications, though sample stability at high CuO concentrations requires attention.

hypotheses.hypothesis: CuO doping enhances optical absorption and radiation shielding while maintaining dielectric stability
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; multifunctional properties demonstrated

title: Expanded Heisenberg Hamiltonians from a Mn/Bi DFT+U study on hexagonal antiferromagnet CaMn2Bi2: excitations and strain-controlled magnetic anisotropy switching
task: Deriving expanded Heisenberg Hamiltonians and studying strain-controlled magnetic anisotropy in CaMn₂Bi₂
key_results: Standard Heisenberg model insufficient; extended model with local magnetization terms accurately describes magnetic excitations; easy-plane anisotropy with strain-tunable preferred in-plane axis switching; SOC-driven gap incorporation via Bi-6p U
comments: DFT+U treatment of both Mn and Bi orbitals improves Mn-Bi hybridization; the strain-tunable anisotropy is promising for magneto-optical control, though switching dynamics and energy barriers require study.

model_io.model: DFT+U with spin-orbit coupling (Mn-3d and Bi-6p Hubbard U)
Extended Heisenberg Hamiltonian with local magnetization terms

hypotheses.hypothesis: Strain controls magnetic anisotropy switching in CaMn₂Bi₂ via SOC-lattice interplay
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; easy-plane anisotropy with tunable in-plane axis demonstrated

title: Spin polarized first principles study of electro-magnetic and optical properties of K2NaXI6 (X :Cr Fe) double halide perovskites
task: Computing electromagnetic and optical properties of K₂NaXI₆ (X=Cr,Fe) double halide perovskites
key_results: K₂NaCrI₆: band gaps 0.8 eV (↑) and 1.8 eV (↓); K₂NaFeI₆: 3.2 eV (↑) and 0.4 eV (↓); ferromagnetic ordering (3 µB Cr, 5 µB Fe); n-type (CrI₆) and p-type (FeI₆) thermoelectric behavior
comments: Spin-polarized calculations reveal substantial spin-splitting useful for spintronics; the large band gap differences between spin channels enable interesting device architectures.

hypotheses.hypothesis: K₂NaXI₆ perovskites exhibit ferromagnetic ordering with spin-dependent band gaps
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; substantial spin-splitting observed

title: Mode localization in chiral periodic approximants of Fibonacci magnonic superlattices
task: Analyzing mode localization in aperiodic Fibonacci magnonic superlattices with chiral interactions
key_results: Flat bands emerge at low frequencies with spatial localization in regions defined by anisotropy, DMI, saturation magnetization contrasts; field-tunable frequency window for flat-band excitation
comments: Deterministic aperiodic ordering elegantly encodes modal selectivity; integration with reconfigurable magnonic devices is promising but requires demonstration of switching dynamics.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Aperiodic Fibonacci modulation creates flat bands with tunable frequency window
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; flat bands localize in material-contrast regions; field tunability demonstrated

title: Domain knowledge-integrated reinforcement learning control of nonlinear tunable vibration absorber under nonstationary excitation
task: Developing domain-knowledge-integrated reinforcement learning for nonlinear vibration absorber control
key_results: Modified Q-learning with Parseval's theorem integration achieves R²=0.994 vs. optimal control baseline; 58% vibration energy reduction; real-time experimental validation under nonstationary excitation
comments: Elegant fusion of vibration domain knowledge with model-free RL reduces computational burden; generalization to multi-degree-of-freedom systems and comparison with adaptive classical controllers needed.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Vibration-domain knowledge accelerates Q-learning convergence for TVA control under nonstationary excitation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; achieves near-optimal performance with reduced computational overhead

title: Interplay between gelation and glass formation in silica nanoparticle colloids
task: Linking drying-induced microstructure evolution to gel-glass transition and rheology
key_results: Continuous transition from percolated gel to dynamically arrested glass driven by drying; scaling law for diffusion coefficient identifies percolation threshold
comments: The SALR interaction framework is well-motivated and the microstructure-rheology linkage is insightful, though experimental validation of the predicted scaling law would be valuable.

model_io.model: Molecular dynamics simulations of silica nanoparticle colloids

hypotheses.hypothesis: Drying-induced pair potential and concentration variations drive gel-to-glass transition
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; continuous transition observed with distinct mechanical responses

title: Dual-mode superconducting diode effect enabled by in-plane and out-of-plane magnetic field
task: Realizing dual-mode superconducting diode effect with independent magnetic field control
key_results: Dual-mode SDE in 2H-NbS2/2H-NbSe2 with similar diode efficiency; out-of-plane and in-plane modes differ by two orders in operational field and temperature dependence
comments: The dual-mode functionality is impressive, though the mechanism of symmetry breaking along multiple orientations requires deeper theoretical understanding.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Both out-of-plane and in-plane fields can independently generate superconducting diode effect
hypotheses.result: Confirmed with similar diode efficiency but distinct operational characteristics

title: Magnetic clusters in the paramagnetic phase of a high-temperature ferromagnetic metal–organic framework
task: Studying magnetic clusters in paramagnetic phase of high-temperature ferromagnetic metal-organic framework
key_results: Cr(tri)2(CF3SO3)0.33 exhibits mesoscopic magnetic correlated clusters in paramagnetic regime with MHz-range slow dynamics; ferromagnetic behavior near ambient temperature; clustering similar to magnetoelectronic phase segregation in manganites
comments: The discovery of cluster precursors in the paramagnetic phase provides insight into the ferromagnetic transition mechanism; connection to colossal magnetoresistance materials is intriguing but speculative.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Magnetic clusters form in paramagnetic phase before ferromagnetic ordering in high-Tc MOF
hypotheses.result: Confirmed by NMR and FMR measurements of slow MHz-range dynamics

title: Magnetically programmable surface acoustic wave filters: device concept and predictive modeling
task: Designing magnetically programmable SAW filters via islet magnetization state control
key_results: Predicted SAW transmission change of 52.0 dB/mm at 3.8 GHz based on Co/Ni islet magnetic alignment; extended energy conservation framework enables modeling of arbitrary magnetization patterns
comments: Clever magnetoelastic approach to SAW filtering with magnetic programmability; experimental demonstration and integration with existing RF filter architectures would validate device feasibility.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Islet magnetic alignment programmability enables selective SAW frequency attenuation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed via simulations; 52 dB/mm change predicted at 3.8 GHz

title: Bound states in the continuum and near-exceptional points in a reflection-based cavity-magnonic system
task: Realizing bound states in continuum and near-exceptional points in cavity-magnonic systems via reflection
key_results: Reflection-based BICs with sharply enhanced group delay; PT-symmetric operating point enabling direction-selective coherent perfect absorption (CPA); coupled magnon-photon interference mechanism
comments: Non-Hermitian phenomena in passive magnonic systems is significant; practical applications to compact wave-control devices require demonstration of tuning bandwidth and robustness.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Interference in reflection geometry produces BICs and PT-symmetric exceptional points in cavity-magnonic systems
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; BICs with enhanced group delay and direction-selective CPA observed

title: Skyrmion quantum diode prototype: bridging micromagnetic simulations and quantum models
task: Bridging micromagnetic simulations and quantum models to design skyrmion quantum diode prototype
key_results: Unidirectional skyrmion transport via skyrmion Hall effect in asymmetric junctions (20–3 nm); flux-tunable quantum architecture compatibility; preliminary anharmonicity insights for skyrmion qubits
comments: Innovative hybrid classical-quantum approach for one-way quantum links; practical integration challenges and noise robustness for scaled systems require further development.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Skyrmion Hall effect enables unidirectional transport suitable for quantum information processing
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; directional control demonstrated down to 3 nm skyrmion diameter

title: Associations of continuous glucose monitor derived time in range and glycaemic variability with diet lifestyle and demographics
task: Assess continuous glucose monitor metrics as predictors of cardiovascular risk in non-diabetic individuals
key_results: Higher time-in-range (TIR 3.9–5.6 mmol/L) associated with lower HbA1c and OGTT glucose; TIR provided moderate discrimination for 10-year ASCVD risk (AUC = 0.75); sleep duration inversely correlated with mean glucose
comments: Exploratory analysis with moderate predictive power; longer-term health outcomes needed to establish clinical utility in euglycemic populations.

hypotheses.hypothesis: CGM metrics correlate with cardiometabolic markers in non-diabetic individuals
hypotheses.result: TIR 3.9–5.6 shows AUC = 0.75 for ASCVD risk discrimination; moderate association with diet and sleep patterns

title: Tumor-infiltrating immature innate lymphoid cells in colorectal cancer are biased toward ILC1/tissue-resident NK cell differentiation
task: Characterize innate lymphoid cell populations and differentiation bias in colorectal cancer and peritoneal metastases
key_results: Primary CRC and CRC-PM tumors depleted of ILC3, enriched for ILC1/trNK cells and cNK cells; naïve ILC (nILC) population transcriptionally skewed toward ILC1/trNK differentiation; co-culture with OP9-DL1 induces nILC-to-ILC1/trNK differentiation
comments: Single-cell RNA-seq and functional assays provide mechanistic insight; ILC1/NK bias may explain impaired anti-tumor immunity in CRC-PM.

model_io.model: Single-cell transcriptomic and flow cytometric analysis of CRC/CRC-PM tumors
inputs: tumor tissue samples (CRC, CRC-PM, healthy colon)
outputs: ILC subset abundance (ILC1, ILC2, ILC3, trNK, cNK)

hypotheses.hypothesis: nILC populations in CRC-PM are biased toward ILC1/trNK differentiation
hypotheses.result: Transcriptional skewing confirmed; OP9-DL1 co-culture induces nILC-to-ILC1/trNK transition
